_doc: Voice-trial manifest — the concrete persona -> engine -> voice mappings for the by-ear trial. Derived from voice/personas.py (the source of truth for the persona records) and voice/engines/REQUIREMENTS.md (the install/port/voice-semantics reference). This file is the trial CONFIG: which character runs on which engine on which port, what 'voice' arg that engine expects, and whether it needs the shared reference clip. It does NOT duplicate the brain/voice_description text (that lives in personas.py and is loaded at runtime via loop.select_persona / loop._voice_arg_for); it records the trial WIRING so the launcher/verify can iterate the five.
voice_base: A refined, educated Australian woman in her early forties. Clear and articulate — NOT broad, country, or ocker. Warm, mid-low pitch; unhurried, composed pace. Dry, understated wit with a sense of refinement and a little mystery. (Same base under all five; only the shading on top changes. Verbatim from personas.VOICE_BASE.)

company_voice_ref_plan.what: COMPANY_VOICE_REF is a real wav of the refined-mature-Australian-female base voice. It does NOT exist yet — no clip was fabricated. The three clone engines (chatterbox, xtts, cosyvoice) FAIL LOUD without it.
company_voice_ref_plan.post_restart_source: Generate it from Qwen3-VoiceDesign (engine qwen3tts, :4128) — no clip needed; it DESIGNS the voice from personas.VOICE_BASE. See voice/ops/make_reference_clip.sh. Use that one wav as COMPANY_VOICE_REF for all three clone engines so every character voices the SAME designed identity.

voice_arg_semantics.qwen3tts: voice = a voice DESCRIPTION (instruct). VoiceDesign builds the voice from text. NO ref clip.
voice_arg_semantics.cosyvoice: voice = a voice DESCRIPTION/instruction (steers style) PLUS a ref clip (COMPANY_VOICE_REF). Needs both.
voice_arg_semantics.orpheus: voice = a NAMED voice from the bank (tara/leah/jess/leo/dan/mia/zac/zoe). NO ref clip.
voice_arg_semantics.chatterbox: voice = a ref-clip path (or None -> COMPANY_VOICE_REF). Needs the clip.
voice_arg_semantics.xtts: voice = a speaker-clip path (or None -> COMPANY_VOICE_REF). Needs the clip. NON-COMMERCIAL weights — trial only.

personas.name: Viv
personas.notes: Warm-realness benchmark; keep COMPANY_CHATTERBOX_EXAGGERATION LOW (0.5 default, lower for the composed register). voice=null -> uses COMPANY_VOICE_REF.

personas.name: Tess
personas.notes: Instruct-coachable + clones the ref clip. loop.py sends the voice_description as the instruct; the wrapper appends <|endofprompt|>. Needs COMPANY_VOICE_REF too.

personas.name: Sable
personas.notes: TRIAL STARTER. VoiceDesign — describe + lock + re-tune. No clip needed. Model already in HF cache. This is the engine to bring up first post-restart.

personas.name: Pip
personas.notes: Cued emotion via inline tags (<laugh>, <chuckle>). Named voice 'tara' (warmest). Heaviest VRAM (vLLM + Llama-3B) — mind co-residency with the chat brain on the 16GB card.

personas.name: Wren
personas.notes: Realism ceiling — most alive read. voice=null -> COMPANY_VOICE_REF. XTTS accepts numeric speed. NON-COMMERCIAL license — by-ear comparison only, never a production path.
